volumeTitle: The Merchant. The Braggart Soldier. The Ghost. The Persian
label: The Merchant (Section 4.1.689 - Section 4.4.749e)

sections.label: Section 4.1.689
sections.translation: By heavens , I believe it .

sections.label: Section 4.1.689b
sections.original: Ei hac mecum , ut videas semul
sections.translation: Step this way with me , that you , my Juno , may see as well your rival Alcmena .

sections.label: Section 4.1.691
sections.original: Ecastor vero istuc eo quantum potest .—
sections.translation: I ’ troth , I certainly shall go there , as fast as I can .

sections.label: Section 4.2.692
sections.original: Parumne ést malai rei , quod amat Demipho ,
sections.translation: Is this too little of a misfortune that Demipho ’ s in love , that he must be extravagant as well ? If he had been inviting ten men of highest rank to dinner ,

sections.label: Section 4.2.695
sections.original: nimium obsonavit . sed coquos , quasi in mari
sections.translation: he has provided too much . But the cooks he directed in such a way just as at sea the time-keeper [*] is wont to direct the rowers . I hired a Cook myself , but I ’ m surprised that he hasn ’ t come as I directed him . But who ’ s this , I wonder , that ’ s coming out of my house ? The door ’ s opening .

sections.label: Section 4.3.700
sections.original: Miserior mulier me nec fiet , nec fuit ,
sections.translation: No woman ever will be , or ever has been , more wretched than myself in being married to such a husband . Alas ! unhappy that I am ! Just see , to what a husband have you committed yourself and the property you have ! Just see , to what a person I brought ten talents for a portion ; that I should see these things , that I should endure these insults .

sections.label: Section 4.3.705
sections.original: Perii hercle , rure iam rediit uxor mea :
sections.translation: I ’ troth , I ’ m undone ; my wife ’ s returned from the country already . I do believe she has seen the damsel in the house . But what she says I cannot distinctly hear from hence ; I ’ ll go nearer to her .

sections.label: Section 4.3.708b
sections.translation: Ah ! woe to wretched me !

sections.label: Section 4.3.708c
sections.translation: Aye , and to me as well .

sections.label: Section 4.3.709
sections.translation: I ’ m utterly undone !

sections.label: Section 4.3.709b
sections.translation: As for me , i ’ faith , to my sorrow I ’ m downright undone ! she has seen her . May all the Gods confound you , Demipho !

sections.label: Section 4.3.711
sections.original: Pol hoc est , ire quod rus meus vir noluit .
sections.translation: I ’ troth , this was it , why my husband wouldn ’ t go into the country .

sections.label: Section 4.3.712
sections.original: Quid nunc ego faciam nisi uti ádeam atque adloquar ?
sections.translation: What shall I do now , but go up and speak to her ? ( Goes up to her .) The husband bids health to his wife . Are the country people [*] becoming townsfolk ?

sections.label: Section 4.3.714b
sections.translation: They are acting more decently than those who are not become country people .

sections.label: Section 4.3.716
sections.translation: Are the people in the country at all in fault ?

sections.label: Section 4.3.716b
sections.translation: I ’ faith , less so than the townsfolk , and much less mischief do they meet with for themselves .

sections.label: Section 4.3.718
sections.original: Quid autem urbani deliquerunt ? dic mihi ,
sections.translation: But in what have the townsfolk done wrong ? Tell me that .

sections.label: Section 4.3.721
sections.translation: i ’ faith , I long to know .

sections.label: Section 4.3.721b
sections.translation: But you are trying me on purpose .

sections.label: Section 4.3.719
sections.translation: Whose woman is that in the house ?

sections.label: Section 4.3.719b
sections.translation: What , have you seen her ?

sections.label: Section 4.3.720
sections.translation: I have seen her .

sections.label: Section 4.3.720b
sections.translation: Whose is she , do you ask ?

sections.label: Section 4.3.720c
sections.translation: I shall find out , in spite of you ;

sections.label: Section 4.3.722
sections.original: Vin dicam quoiast ? illa — illa edepol — vae mihi ,
sections.translation: Do you wish me to tell you whose she is ? She , she — ( Aside .) Ah me ! upon my faith , I don ’ t know what to say .

sections.label: Section 4.3.723b
sections.translation: Do you hesitate ?

sections.label: Section 4.3.723c
sections.translation: I never saw one who did it more .

sections.label: Section 4.3.724
sections.translation: But why don ’ t you tell me ?

sections.label: Section 4.3.724b
sections.translation: Nay , but if I may —

sections.label: Section 4.3.724c
sections.translation: You ought to tell me .

sections.label: Section 4.3.725
sections.original: Non possum , ita instas ; urges quasi pro noxio .
sections.translation: I cannot , you hurry me so ; you press me as though were guilty .

sections.label: Section 4.3.726
sections.translation: I know you are free from all guilt .

sections.label: Section 4.3.726b
sections.translation: Speak out as boldly as you please .

sections.label: Section 4.3.727
sections.translation: Tell me , then .

sections.label: Section 4.3.727b
sections.translation: I , tell you ?

sections.label: Section 4.3.727c
sections.translation: Why , it must be told , in spite of everything .

sections.label: Section 4.3.728
sections.translation: She is — Do you wish me tell her name as well ?

sections.label: Section 4.3.728b
sections.translation: You are trifling . I ’ ve caught you in the fact ; you are guilty .

sections.label: Section 4.3.729b
sections.translation: Guilty of what ?

sections.label: Section 4.3.730
sections.translation: Why , this same woman is —

sections.label: Section 4.3.730b
sections.translation: Who is she ?

sections.label: Section 4.3.730c
sections.translation: She —

sections.label: Section 4.3.731
sections.original: Iam — si nihil usus esset , iam non dicerem .
sections.translation: If now I had no occasion for silence , now I shouldn ’ t tell you [*] .

sections.label: Section 4.3.732_735
sections.original: Non tu scis quae sit illa ?
sections.translation: Marry , come up ! don ’ t you know who she is ?

sections.label: Section 4.3.732_735b
sections.translation: Why , yes , I do know . I ’ ve been chosen as an arbitrator with respect to her .

sections.label: Section 4.3.736b
sections.translation: An arbitrator ? Now I know ; you have invited her here to consult with you .

sections.label: Section 4.3.738
sections.translation: Why no ; she has been given me as a deposit .

sections.label: Section 4.3.738b
sections.translation: I understand .

sections.label: Section 4.3.739
sections.translation: By my troth , it ’ s not anything of that sort .

sections.label: Section 4.3.739b
sections.translation: You are clearing yourself too soon [*] .

sections.label: Section 4.3.740
sections.original: Nimium negoti repperi . enim vero haereo .
sections.translation: Too much of a business have I met with ; really I ’ m stuck fast .

sections.label: Section 4.4.741
sections.original: Agite ite actutum , nam mi amatori seni
sections.translation: Make haste , get quickly on , for I ’ ve got to cook a dinner for an old gentleman in love . And , in fact , when I think of it again , it ’ s to be cooked for ourselves , not him for whom we ’ ve been hired : for a person that ’ s in love , if he has that with which he is in love , he esteems that as food ,

sections.label: Section 4.4.745
sections.translation: to see her , embrace her , kiss her , chat with her ; but we , I trust , shall return well laden home . Step this way . But see , here ’ s the old gentleman that hired us .

sections.label: Section 4.4.748
sections.translation: Why , look ! I ’ m undone ! here ’ s the Cook .

sections.label: Section 4.4.748b
sections.translation: We are come .

sections.label: Section 4.4.749
sections.translation: Be off !

sections.label: Section 4.4.749b
sections.translation: How , be off ?

sections.label: Section 4.4.749c
sections.translation: Hush ! Be off !

sections.label: Section 4.4.749d
sections.translation: What , I , be off ?

sections.label: Section 4.4.749e
sections.translation: Be off , I say .
